content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of non-negative integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. At most once, you may choose a non-negative integer XXX and for each valid iii, change AiA_iAi​ to Ai⊕XA_i \oplus XAi​⊕X (⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR).

Find the minimum possible value of the sum of all elements of the resulting sequence.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum possible sum.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: If we choose X=6X = 6X=6, the sequence becomes (4,5,2,3,0)(4, 5, 2, 3, 0)(4,5,2,3,0).
Example case 2: We can choose X=7X = 7X=7 to make all elements of the resulting sequence equal to 000.
Example case 3: We can choose X=1X = 1X=1. The sequence becomes (0,0,2)(0, 0, 2)(0,0,2), with sum 222.
